title: Display actual dates and timestamps rather than relative
excerpt: My company just moved over to Monday.com. Part of the goal is to move away from outlook, moving messages into Monday so that everyone has access to the information. I use the message column to back-track, on what a clien…

posts.author: lizuare (Liz Fitzwater)
posts.cookedHtml:
My company just moved over to Monday.com. Part of the goal is to move away from outlook, moving messages into Monday so that everyone has access to the information. I use the message column to back-track, on what a client said and when. I also use the global search function to look at what I have done for the week for billing purposes, etc. It drives me crazy that the time stamp on these events are relative (2 days ago, 4 hours ago, etc). It makes it nearly impossible to figure out who did what when, Especially if it is beyond the current week. I would love to have the option to have it say the day of week and date (Tuesday 8/25 9:38am). This would be so much more helpful when I have to look back on the history of events.

Thanks!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Sounds like a great idea for a column setting. Display actual date and time or Display relative times. Even better if we can choose the exact date and time formats.

posts.author: Craigness (Craig Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Where does this feature stand in development? It’s been over a year since it was brought up

posts.author: brian_casecraft (Brian Judd)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this feature. Our projects span over months and this relative time track is not helpful.

posts.author: MichWing (Michelle Winger)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvote - this feature would be super helpful. Date/time stamps for audit trailing updates are crucial.

posts.author: Lori.of.Capstone (Lori McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, for our audit trail, having a date and timestamp on Updates would be invaluable.

posts.author: helenstrafe (Helen Abbott)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this also please! Or at least to have a choice. It’s so unhelpful having to hover on ‘17d ago’ to find the actual dates. Please Monday! Change this!

posts.author: Tree (Tree)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. Relative dates are useless. Since Monday can calculate the relativity of the date, it’s clear that the actual date & time is available. Please display the actual date & time rather than the date difference.

posts.author: cwersal (Cari Wersal)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree this is a necessary feature. We struggle with the “friendly” dates as we track interactions with clients and need to provide detailed summaries with facts. Where is this on the list of enhancements? Thanks.

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everybody, Tom from monday here. I completely understand this need, and I hope we’ll be able to deliver in the future! In full transparency, it’s currently not on the roadmap, but I wanted to offer an interim solution by using our Formula column’s “FORMAT_DATE()” function, for example:

FORMAT_DATE({Date}, "MM-DD-YYYY")

image1182×590 42.4 KB

You can also use the “hide” option on the native date column to avoid duplicate columns and keep a cleaner board view

Hope that helps!
